Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

while reviewing the changes to apex-pdp, we made the decision that, when responding to PAP, the PDPs should only send back the original deployment list (with failures removed), instead of sending back the complete list of policies.  I don't think I saw that change here.  Maybe that will be done in a separate review?

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> while reviewing the changes to apex-pdp, we made the decision that, when responding to PAP, the PDPs should only send back the original deployment list (with failures removed), instead of sending back the complete list of policies.  I don't think I saw that change here.  Maybe that will be done in a separate review?

I didn't realize it in the apex review,  I have some follow up questions/concerns: 

1.
We are lacking documentation for this feature, and in general the process to grow a PDP type from PAP perspective, what is expected of a new type of PDP to comply with to be recognized by PAP.

2.
What needs to be sent back exactly in the response, the comment refers to the deploy list but there are 2 lists, deploy, and undeploy, that could experiment failures.

3.
What's the reason PAP requires this changes wrt current behavior, how does PAP handles the response and acts upon failures reported on it?

4.
What are the rules for STATUS messages triggered from heartbeat, any changes related to the policy list being sent out?   It may have been good to have an specific RESPONSE message to avoid overloading the STATUS message processing conditional logic to act differently depending in context.

5.
In "legacy" processing, PAP could be sync'ed with PDP status at all times as the STATUS messages contain the list of policy ids.    How consistency is achieved by the PAP with the delta policy?   Does the UPDATE "policies" field need to be used for any purpose, perhaps re-synchronization, etc .. or operator command to resync a PDP with PAP?

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> > 
> > while reviewing the changes to apex-pdp, we made the decision that, when responding to PAP, the PDPs should only send back the original deployment list (with failures removed), instead of sending back the complete list of policies.  I don't think I saw that change here.  Maybe that will be done in a separate review?
> 
> I didn't realize it in the apex review,  I have some follow up questions/concerns: 
> 
> 1.
> We are lacking documentation for this feature, and in general the process to grow a PDP type from PAP perspective, what is expected of a new type of PDP to comply with to be recognized by PAP.
> 
> 2.
> What needs to be sent back exactly in the response, the comment refers to the deploy list but there are 2 lists, deploy, and undeploy, that could experiment failures.
> 
> 3.
> What's the reason PAP requires this changes wrt current behavior, how does PAP handles the response and acts upon failures reported on it?
> 
> 4.
> What are the rules for STATUS messages triggered from heartbeat, any changes related to the policy list being sent out?   It may have been good to have an specific RESPONSE message to avoid overloading the STATUS message processing conditional logic to act differently depending in context.
> 
> 5.
> In "legacy" processing, PAP could be sync'ed with PDP status at all times as the STATUS messages contain the list of policy ids.    How consistency is achieved by the PAP with the delta policy?   Does the UPDATE "policies" field need to be used for any purpose, perhaps re-synchronization, etc .. or operator command to resync a PDP with PAP?

All good questions.  Here are my thoughts:
1) Created https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-3264 to document it.
2) What makes sense is to send back the original deploy list, with failures removed.  To identify failures in the undeploy list, those policies that are still deployed can be added to the returned deploy list.
3) To shorten the lists of policies being transmitted back and forth, we made the decision that PDPs do not have to send back a complete list of the deployed policies.  This requires a minor change in PAP to only compare the result with the original deploy & undeploy lists.  One side effect of this is that it would still work even if a PDP chose to send back the complete list of deployed policies each time.  (In other words, you can ignore my original comment - drools-pdp can continue to return the complete list of policies.  For that matter, if we decide to support multiple PAPs in this release, we may need the PDPs to continue sending complete lists so that all of the PAPs remain in sync regardless of which one initiated the update.)
4) No changes; heartbeat messages should continue to send complete lists of policies.  In the future, if there are lots of policies deployed to a given PDP type, we may need to have two different heartbeat messages, one short and frequent, one longer and less frequent.
5) The UPDATE "policies" field will be deprecated; deploy & undeploy should be used instead.  These fields are sufficient for resynchronization.
